notes:
The Zoom release: focus on any block as its own page, and re-shape your
outline by dragging.

**New**

- **Zoom into any block — "block as page."** Click a block's gutter marker (or
  press `⌘.`) to focus it: the block becomes an editable page title, its
  children become the body, and an ancestor breadcrumb shows where you are.
  `⌘⇧.` zooms back out, restoring your scroll position. Zoom is a view, never a
  data change — your note and its Markdown export are untouched. (PRD Addendum 20
  §T1–T6: *"model is block-as-page; zoom is a view, never a data change."*)
- **Block backlinks in the focused view.** A zoomed block now lists the blocks
  that reference it, with a count — the same backlinks you'd expect on a page,
  scoped to that block. (Addendum 20 §T4.)
- **One clean gutter affordance per block.** Hovering a block reveals a single
  marker in the left gutter — a bulleted block uses its own bullet, everything
  else a faint dot — that zooms on click and reveals a rotating collapse chevron
  on parents. (PRD Addendum 19 §S2, Option C.)
- **Drag a block by its marker to move it.** Press the gutter marker and drag:
  vertical position picks where it lands, horizontal position picks the depth —
  drag right to nest under the block above, left to outdent. The dragged block
  and its children dim while you drag, and a drop line shows exactly where it'll
  go. A clean click still zooms. (Addendum 19 §S2.3.)
- **Pasting text with `[[links]]`, `#tags`, or `((refs))` re-creates them.**
  Pasting plain text that contains wikilink, tag, or block-reference syntax now
  reconstitutes the live chips instead of leaving raw brackets.

**Under the hood**

- The gutter marker + chevron render as a hover overlay *outside* the editable
  content, not as an in-content widget — a `contentEditable=false` widget at a
  block's start broke caret motion and selection in WKWebView (Safari). The
  overlay keeps select-to-line-start and wrap-on-selection working on both
  engines.
- Drag-to-reparent applies one re-parent on the flat block model (the same shape
  the database round-trips), so it works uniformly for paragraph-child blocks and
  list items and leaves collapse state alone. The operation is bounded and
  refuses any move that would form a `parent_id` cycle.
